top_strategic.title: Sponsorship whitespace: 4 equipment categories with ZERO brand detection
top_strategic.evidence: The camera sees these items but we detect no brands. ZERO detections: accessories, apparel, footwear, wristband. Near-zero: sunglasses(2). Each gap = a brand that could be paying for visibility data.
top_strategic.action: Add shoe/apparel/sunglasses brand detection examples to Gemini prompt — immediate revenue unlock

top_strategic.title: Longitudinal tracking possible: 61 players with 5+ clips
top_strategic.evidence: 61 players have enough data for improvement tracking. Top: PickleBill(394), Chintan(179), Coach_Block(131), Tmf(124), Daniella(93)
top_strategic.action: Build player improvement trajectory product — track skill changes over time

top_strategic.title: Sponsorship whitespace detected in 4155 clips (97.0%) — sell to brands
top_strategic.evidence: We detect unbranded equipment/space in 4155 clips. This is the gap between what's visible and what's branded — sellable data.
top_strategic.action: Package sponsorship whitespace data as 'brand opportunity report' for equipment companies

top_strategic.title: Endorsement intelligence: 151 player-brand pairs with 3+ co-occurrences
top_strategic.evidence: Top pairs: PickleBill-JOOLA(643x), Chintan-JOOLA(289x), Tmf-JOOLA(271x), Coach_Block-JOOLA(233x), Daniella-JOOLA(197x)
top_strategic.action: Build 'brand ambassador identification' product for equipment companies

top_strategic.title: Social caption generation at 99.9% — ready to productize
top_strategic.evidence: Social media captions are generated for 4280/4283 clips. This is already a viable content-as-a-service product.
top_strategic.action: Create API endpoint for social caption generation — charge per clip

top_data.title: Viral shot: 'overhead' clips average 5.12 viral vs 'none' at 1
top_data.evidence: Shot viral averages: overhead=5.12, speed_up=4.27, reset=3.87, volley=3.52, dink=2.92, drive=2.87, serve=2.18, unknown=1.14, none=1
top_data.action: Prioritize highlight reels featuring high-viral shot types

top_data.title: Prompt fix: 5 badges consistently missed — add positive examples
top_data.evidence: Production awards these but DaaS misses them: 'rally master' (6 missed), 'new look' (5 missed), 'steady eddie' (4 missed), 'fresh fit' (3 missed), 'paddle tap celebration' (3 missed). These are real badges that our prompt doesn't detect.
top_data.action:
Add positive examples to Gemini prompt:
'When analyzing badges, make sure to check for these commonly missed badges:
  - rally master: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - new look: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - steady eddie: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - fresh fit: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - paddle tap celebration: Look for [describe visual pattern]

top_data.title: DaaS badge prediction: F1=0.98 (precision=0.99, recall=0.97)
top_data.evidence: Across 429 verified clips: 1478 correct predictions, 16 false positives, 41 missed badges. Overall F1=0.981.
top_data.action: Target recall improvement: add more badge examples to Gemini prompt

top_data.title: Signature moves = viral gold: 4253 clips with signatures avg 2.9 viral vs 3.9
top_data.evidence: 4253 clips have identified signature moves, averaging 2.9 viral vs 3.9 for clips without.
top_data.action: Auto-tag signature move clips as premium content

top_data.title: Net/venue branding inflates 7 brands — true share-of-court differs from raw counts
top_data.evidence: Brands with significant venue-only detections (not product use): JOOLA (551/6941 are venue), Nicol (6/14 are venue), PUSHIT (5/9 are venue), Onix (4/45 are venue), adidas (4/47 are venue). Raw counts overstate market share. Product-use only: JOOLA=6390, Nike=683, PICKLEBALL=235, Reolink=132, HEAD=120.
top_data.action: Report TRUE share-of-court (product-use only) to brand partners, not raw detection counts

all_findings.title: Viral formula: quality (7.39 vs 4.46) + watchability (6.78 vs 2.47)
all_findings.evidence: Top 100 viral clips: quality=7.39, watchability=6.78. Bottom 100: quality=4.46, watchability=2.47. Both metrics track strongly with viral potential.
all_findings.action: Use (quality + watchability) / 2 as quick viral prediction score

all_findings.title: Rally length matters: long rallies are more viral (long=3.1 vs short=2.2)
all_findings.evidence: Avg rally: 15.6 shots. Long rallies (15+): 2181 clips, avg viral 3.1. Short (4-): 65 clips, avg viral 2.2.
all_findings.action: Use rally length as a quality signal in highlight curation

all_findings.title: Prompt fix: JOOLA appears in 310/429 verified clips — add disambiguation
all_findings.evidence: JOOLA dominates 72% of clips. Prompt should distinguish: paddle logo (real JOOLA use), net logo (JOOLA sponsors nets), and venue branding (JOOLA sponsors the facility).
all_findings.action:
Add to brand detection prompt:
'For JOOLA specifically, classify detection source:
  - paddle: JOOLA logo on paddle face = confirmed product use
  - net: JOOLA logo on net = venue sponsorship, NOT product use
  - venue: JOOLA on walls/banners = facility sponsorship
Only count paddle detections as brand_category: paddle'

all_findings.action: Build 'brand ambassador identification' product for equipment companies

all_findings.evidence: Story arc 'athletic' appears in 22.0% of top-viral clips but only 0.0% of bottom-viral clips.
all_findings.action: Prioritize 'athletic' clips in automated highlight reels

product_concept.name: EquipmentGap Scout
product_concept.first_screen: Dashboard showing 4 equipment categories (shoes, paddles, apparel, bags) with 0% brand detection. Each category has a red 'Unsponsored' badge with CTA 'View opportunity.' Clicking reveals 5 missed detection examples side-by-side with corrected annotations, letting sponsors see exactly what their brand is losing.
product_concept.why_powerful: Turns invisible sponsorship gaps into concrete, visual proof that directly feeds prompt engineers the ground-truth examples needed to fix detection blind spots.

connections.strategic_finding: Sponsorship whitespace: 4 equipment categories with ZERO brand detection
connections.data_finding: Net/venue branding inflates 7 brands — true share-of-court differs from raw counts
connections.combined_insight: Combine 'Sponsorship whitespace: 4 equipment cate' with 'Net/venue branding inflates 7 brands — t' for a targeted product.

product_concept.name: True Share Analyzer
product_concept.first_screen: Split-screen comparison view: left shows '7 Brands Inflated by Venue/Net' (raw counts ranked), right shows 'Corrected Court Share %' (adjusted for venue bias). Users toggle between a specific court venue, filtering out net/signage noise from true equipment sponsorship. Includes a 'Reality Gap' percentage for each brand.
product_concept.why_powerful: Prevents sponsors from overpaying for inflated brand-detection numbers by isolating actual equipment placement from environmental noise—a direct competitor intelligence tool.

product_concept.name: JOOLA Clarity Engine
product_concept.first_screen: Search bar with 'JOOLA' pre-filled, showing '310 of 429 verified clips (72%).' Below: three filter tabs—'Paddle Brand,' 'Court Signage,' 'Ambiguous.' Clicking each tab shows clips grouped by detection context, with a toggle to 'Train model on this distinction' for the prompt engineer to action.
product_concept.why_powerful: Disambiguates high-frequency false positives at scale by letting analysts categorize exactly where JOOLA is *actually* branded vs. coincidental, feeding surgical prompt refinements that improve precision across all detections.
